build_approach.rationale: Armbian uefi-x86 already provides GPT/EFI image, kernel integration, and SBC-style build automation. Atomic Pi support is an overlay (userpatches/customize-image.sh) rather than a new board port.

hardware_notes.audio: XMOS xCORE USB audio → TI TAS5719 class-D amp → powered outputs. GPIO 349 deasserted at boot by atomicpi-hold-xmos.service.

stages.name: armbian-base-build
stages.description: Armbian compile.sh builds the uefi-x86 Trixie base image

stages.name: customize-image
stages.description: armbian/userpatches/customize-image.sh applies Atomic Pi hardware layer

stages.name: flash-and-test
stages.description: Flash to eMMC, run firstboot.sh, verify test-matrix.md

pivot_history.reason: Armbian already maintains uefi-x86 target with Trixie, Standard support tier. Provides SBC workflow (flashable images, kernel/rootfs integration, GitHub Actions) without reinventing image creation.

notes.msg: XMOS USB device name in asound.conf uses card name 'XMOS'. Must verify against 'aplay -l' output on first live boot. The card name comes from USB descriptor strings and could vary.
PulseAudio default-sink.pa uses a guessed PA sink name. Run 'pactl list sinks short' on first boot and update files/default-sink.pa if the name differs.

customize-image.sh git-clones digitalloggers DKMS modules during Armbian build — requires network in chroot. Armbian build normally has internet access. If offline, pre-clone into armbian/userpatches/packages/ and update the script path.

Armbian uefi-x86 BRANCH=current kernel version is unknown until build runs. If the kernel ships without CONFIG_GPIOLIB_SYSFS or similar, the sysfs GPIO approach in the systemd services will fail and we'll need to switch to libgpiod/gpiod-based services.

PACKAGE_LIST_ADDITIONAL in config-atomicpi-server.conf includes 'linux-headers-current-x86' — Armbian's header package naming convention. Verify this is the correct package name for the uefi-x86 board after first build.
